message:
refactor(agentmail): return structured results from MarkMessageRead and AcknowledgeMessage APIs

Continues the pattern from the ReleaseReservations refactor: message read/ack
operations now return structured result types instead of bare errors, giving
callers access to server-side timestamps and confirmation details.

New types (types.go):
- MessageReadResult: message_id, read bool, read_at timestamp
- MessageAckResult: message_id, acknowledged bool, acknowledged_at/read_at timestamps

Interface change (unified.go):
- agentMailClient.MarkMessageRead: error → (*MessageReadResult, error)
- agentMailClient.AcknowledgeMessage: error → (*MessageAckResult, error)
- UnifiedMessenger.Read() updated to destructure (_, err) from MarkMessageRead

Handler updates (serve/mail.go):
- handleMarkRead and handleAcknowledge now return structured JSON responses
  with timestamps instead of bare success booleans

Tests:
- tools_httptest_test.go: MarkMessageRead/AcknowledgeMessage tests now verify
  result fields (read, message_id, timestamps)
- serve/server_test.go: new handler tests for structured responses

Also updates beads issue bd-j9jo3.3.5 (redaction adapter) from open to
in_progress, and closes bd-j9jo3.4.2 (durable event publication from adapters).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -474,9 +474,9 @@ func runMailMark(cmd *cobra.Command, session, agent string, action mailAction, i
 		var markErr error
 		switch action {
 		case mailActionRead:
-			markErr = client.MarkMessageRead(ctx, projectKey, agent, id)
+			_, markErr = client.MarkMessageRead(ctx, projectKey, agent, id)
 		case mailActionAck:
-			markErr = client.AcknowledgeMessage(ctx, projectKey, agent, id)
+			_, markErr = client.AcknowledgeMessage(ctx, projectKey, agent, id)
 		}
 		if markErr != nil {
 			errs++
